signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) vs USD -0.872% (n=7) sets up a strong bilateral theme; the 1.953% G8 spread and USD's 290bp yield advantage over JPY create a complex setup — JPY's 1W momentum runs against the yield gap, setting up squeeze risk if USD/JPY falls. The next BoJ meeting is on 28 Apr, which may resolve this setup.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation + ECB Hold
signals.text: Trump's tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure on EUR/USD toward 1.16; EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces this bias. The ECB's next meeting on 30 Apr, with a market bias toward hold, may further influence this setup, especially if the ECB maintains its current stance.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias + Carry
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA hike bias (40% priced) sustains the bid, and the +35bp carry advantage over USD is likely to support AUD/USD, but any disappointment in the RBA's next meeting on 5 May triggers the unwind.

signals.title: USD/CAD — BoC Hold + Oil Price
signals.text: USD/CAD closed at 1.3588 Friday, with CAD COT short -54.7k; the next BoC meeting on 29 Apr, with a market bias toward hold, and the recent drop in WTI oil price is likely to influence USD/CAD, especially if the BoC maintains its current stance.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold + Logan Comments
signals.text: GBP/USD closed at 1.3575 Friday, with GBP COT long +32.6k; Fed's Logan comments on the potential for a rate cut or hike, and the next BoE meeting on 30 Apr, with a market bias toward hold, is likely to influence GBP/USD, particularly if the BoE's stance aligns with market expectations.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short −16.8k, setting up a divergence with the RBNZ's hike bias, where 40% of a hike is priced. This creates a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling. The unwind of these positions is likely to be catalyzed by the next RBNZ meeting on 27 May.
